untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of fundamental report events and retracement percentage calculations (25-50% of initial move), which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The rule also requires multi-bar pattern recognition (initial reaction → retracement → second leg confirmation) that cannot be precisely encoded without event-detection and historical price comparison operators.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using price action patterns: entry requires close beyond previous high/low (simulating second leg confirmation) with above-normal volume. Exit uses pivot points as proxy for retracement levels. However, the core logic—detecting the fundamental report event, measuring retracement percentage of initial move, and confirming a reversal pattern—cannot be fully implemented without event APIs and complex multi-bar lookback functions. A production implementation would require custom event handling and retracement calculation logic external to the standard indicator set.
